children.heading: Agency
children.label: 1 Agency
children.text: The term “agency” has the meaning given that term in .section 7902(a) of title 5

children.heading: Energy Star product
children.label: 2 Energy Star product
children.text: The term “Energy Star product” means a product that is rated for energy efficiency under an Energy Star program.

children.heading: Energy Star program
children.label: 3 Energy Star program
children.text: The term “Energy Star program” means the program established by .section 6294a of this title

children.heading: FEMP designated product
children.label: 4 FEMP designated product
children.text: The term “FEMP designated product” means a product that is designated under the Federal Energy Management Program of the Department of Energy as being among the highest 25 percent of equivalent products for energy efficiency.

children.heading: Product
children.label: 5 Product
children.text: The term “product” does not include any energy consuming product or system designed or procured for combat or combat-related missions.

children.heading: Procurement of energy efficient products
children.label: b Procurement of energy efficient products
A
children.text: an Energy Star product; or

children.label: B
children.text: a FEMP designated product.

children.label: A
children.text: an Energy Star product or FEMP designated product is not cost-effective over the life of the product taking energy cost savings into account; or

children.label: B
children.text: no Energy Star product or FEMP designated product is reasonably available that meets the functional requirements of the agency.

children.heading: Procurement planning
children.label: 3 Procurement planning
children.text: The head of an agency shall incorporate into the specifications for all procurements involving energy consuming products and systems, including guide specifications, project specifications, and construction, renovation, and services contracts that include provision of energy consuming products and systems, and into the factors for the evaluation of offers received for the procurement, criteria for energy efficiency that are consistent with the criteria used for rating Energy Star products and for rating FEMP designated products.

children.heading: Listing of energy efficient products in Federal catalogs
children.label: c Listing of energy efficient products in Federal catalogs
children.text: Energy Star products and FEMP designated products shall be clearly identified and prominently displayed in any inventory or listing of products by the General Services Administration or the Defense Logistics Agency. The General Services Administration or the Defense Logistics Agency shall list in their catalogues, represent as available, and supply only Energy Star products or FEMP designated products for all product categories covered by the Energy Star program or the Federal Energy Management Program, except in cases in which the head of the agency ordering a product specifies in writing that no Energy Star product or FEMP designated product is available to meet the buyer’s functional requirements, or that no Energy Star product or FEMP designated product is cost-effective for the intended application over the life of the product, taking energy cost savings into account.

In the case of electric motors of 1 to 500 horsepower, agencies shall select only premium efficient motors that meet a standard designated by the Secretary. The Secretary shall designate such a standard not later than 120 days after , after considering the recommendations of associated electric motor manufacturers and energy efficiency groups.August 8, 2005

children.label: A
children.text: determined by the Secretary to be effective in increasing the efficiency of air conditioning and refrigeration equipment without having an adverse impact on air conditioning performance (including cooling capacity) or equipment useful life;

children.label: B
children.text: determined by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to be environmentally safe; and

children.label: C
children.text: shown to increase seasonal energy efficiency ratio (SEER) or energy efficiency ratio (EER) when tested by the National Institute of Standards and Technology according to Department of Energy test procedures without causing any adverse impact on the system, system components, the refrigerant or lubricant, or other materials in the system.Results of testing described in subparagraph (C) shall be published in the Federal Register for public review and comment. For purposes of this section, a hardware device or primary refrigerant shall not be considered an additive.

children.heading: Federally-procured appliances with standby power
children.label: e Federally-procured appliances with standby power
i
children.text: uses external standby power devices; or

children.label: ii
children.text: contains an internal standby power function; and

children.label: B
children.text: is included on the list compiled under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: an eligible product that uses not more than 1 watt in the standby power consuming mode of the eligible product; or

children.label: B
children.text: if an eligible product described in subparagraph (A) is not available, the eligible product with the lowest available standby power wattage in the standby power consuming mode of the eligible product.

children.label: i
children.text: lifecycle cost-effective; and

children.label: ii
children.text: practicable; and

children.label: B
children.text: the utility and performance of the eligible product is not compromised by the lower wattage requirement.

children.heading: Eligible products
children.label: 4 Eligible products
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, and the Administrator of General Services, shall compile a publicly accessible list of cost-effective eligible products that shall be subject to the purchasing requirements of paragraph (2).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall issue guidelines to carry out this section.August 8, 2005
